entity.summary: Starknet-based decentralized perpetual swaps exchange that launched its OG Trade mainnet product in January 2024. On July 30, 2024, founder Eduard Jubany Tur announced discontinuation after the protocol failed to find an economically viable path amid low engagement, falling trading activity, weak revenue, and high operating costs.
entity.notes: HEI uses voluntary_shutdown because the operator deliberately discontinued the protocol after concluding that continued operation was not economically viable. The July 30 announcement stated that markets were delisted, positions were closed, and users could withdraw during the wind-down period.

events.title: ZKX OG Trade launched on mainnet
events.description: ZKX launched its gamified perpetual swaps exchange OG Trade on Starknet mainnet.
events.notes: Exact date follows the official ZKX launch post.

events.title: ZKX protocol discontinued
events.description: ZKX announced discontinuation, delisted all markets, closed positions, and began a withdrawal period after concluding that no economically viable path remained.
events.notes: The announcement date is used as the terminal operating marker because markets and positions were closed at that point.

evidence.title: ZKX OG Trade LIVE on Mainnet
evidence.notes: Official launch post states that OG Trade was live on Starknet mainnet on January 31, 2024.

evidence.title: Important statement on discontinuation of ZKX
evidence.notes: Founder statement announces discontinuation, market delisting, position closure, and the withdrawal process.

evidence.title: Crypto futures DEX ZKX announces suspension of operations
evidence.notes: Report preserves the founder's statement and summarizes the low-engagement, low-volume, revenue, cost, and token-value reasons for the shutdown.

evidence.title: StarkNet derivative trading protocol ZKX has ceased operations
evidence.notes: Independent report confirms the founder's statement that the protocol could not find an economically viable path.
